.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)",

NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

.ddx/executions/20260503T025720-bb59ccca/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract",

.ddx/executions/20260502T204932-0e555908/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract",
